# Social Connectedness Index map maker — social-connectedness.org

A free, browser-based site for mapping and exploring the Social Connectedness
Index (SCI), a measure of the relative probability of Facebook friendship
between places. Four client-side tools share static generated data:

- /generator — Map Maker: makes a static map image (PNG/JPG/SVG, plus an MP4
  reel) for one "home" region's friendship pattern, or a two-region comparison.
- /explore   — Interactive Explorer: a MapLibre globe; click a country/region
  to recolor the world by its SCI. It runs with no third-party basemap tiles by
  default. (No programmatic API; human/visual use.)
- /cluster   — Connected Communities: groups a region's sub-national units into
  clusters by Facebook connectedness (-log(SCI) distance, population-weighted
  average-linkage hierarchical clustering, exact K cuts with only tiny
  non-contiguous visual fragments tidied). Pick a regional grouping or single
  country (precomputed, instant) or a custom country combination (clustered live
  in-browser), choose how many clusters K, optionally animate the 1→K split
  sequence; PNG/SVG/MP4 downloads. (No programmatic API; human/visual use.)
- /cgfr      — Cross-Gender Friending Ratio visualizer: a MapLibre globe for
  country and GADM-best regional CGFR values, sharing the site's place geometry
  and search aliases. It runs with no third-party basemap tiles by default. (No
  programmatic API; human/visual use.)

Everything runs client-side; there is no server-side API. The Map Maker can,
however, be driven programmatically in two ways — both work in a headless browser
(e.g. Playwright/Puppeteer) and neither affects ordinary visitors.

## 1. Shareable URL parameters (/generator?...)

Open the generator with query params to pre-fill the controls. Add `autogenerate=1`
to render immediately, and `download=png|jpg|svg|mp4` to also save the file
(download implies autogenerate). With no params the page behaves normally.

Example — friendships FROM Los Angeles County shown across all US counties,
auto-rendered (the home region is a county, so origin=us_county):
  https://social-connectedness.org/generator?origin=us_county&dest=us_county&sourceName=Los%20Angeles&autogenerate=1

Parameters (all optional):
- mode          single | compare                      (default single)
- origin        level id of the home region           (see "levels" below)
- dest          level id to color across the map
- source        exact region id for the home region   (preferred; unambiguous)
- sourceName    region name to look up instead of id  (e.g. "Berlin")
- sourceB / sourceBName   second region (mode=compare)
- regions       what to show: all | same-country | same-subcontinent |
                a continent group OR sub-regional grouping name (e.g. Europe,
                Nordic, "Gulf (GCC)", Andean, Balkans) | ISO2 codes — comma-separated
                (e.g. regions=Europe  or  regions=Nordic  or  regions=US,CA,MX)
- metro         CBSA code to restrict a US ZIP map to one metro area
- palette       single-map palette name (see SCI.listPalettes())
- comparePaletteA / comparePaletteB   palette name per side (mode=compare; same
                names as single palettes; the diverging map runs A -> white -> B;
                defaults Red / Blue)
- refMode       quantile | absolute        (how the SCI scale is anchored)
- refq          reference quantile, 0-1    (default 0.25)
- refval        absolute SCI value         (used when refMode=absolute)
- breakScheme   quantile | even | log | custom
- breaks        custom legend breaks, e.g. breaks=1,2,5,10,25
- compareBreaks custom multipliers for comparison maps
- borders       1|0   show state/province borders     (default 1)
- highlight     1|0   color the home region, including across origin/dest levels
                (default 0)
- title, subtitle      text (auto-generated title if omitted)
- width, height, dpi   output size in inches / DPI     (default 30 x 25 @ 300)
- xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax   explicit map bounds (all four required together)
- autogenerate  1|0   render after applying params
- download      png | jpg | svg | mp4   save the result (implies autogenerate)

Levels (origin/dest ids): country, gadm1 (State/Province), gadm2 (Region),
us_county, us_cbsa (US Metro Area), us_zcta (US ZIP Code). Not every origin pairs
with every dest — call SCI.listLevels() for the valid graph.

## 2. window.SCI JavaScript API (for headless automation)

The generator page exposes `window.SCI`. Await `window.SCI.ready` first. Methods:

- await SCI.ready                         resolves when the page is loaded
- await SCI.config(cfg)                   apply a config object (same keys as the
                                          URL params above; camelCase, real types)
- await SCI.generate(cfg?)               apply cfg (optional) then render; resolves
                                          to the <canvas>, rejects on error
- await SCI.toBlob("png"|"jpg"|"svg"|"mp4")   the current map as a Blob (best for
                                          automation — no browser download dialog)
- await SCI.dataUrl(fmt)                  the current map as a data: URL
- SCI.download(fmt)                       trigger a normal browser download
- await SCI.findRegions(query, {origin, dest, limit})
                                          resolve a name to candidate {id,label,country}
- SCI.listTypes() / SCI.listLevels() / SCI.listPalettes() / SCI.listGroups()
- SCI.reset()

The page also fires a DOM event `sci:generated` (detail: {type,width,height}) on
each successful render, and `sci:error` (detail: {message}) on failure — so
automation can await a render instead of polling.

Batch example (10 regions → 10 PNGs), inside the loaded /generator page:
  await window.SCI.ready;
  const names = ["Los Angeles", "Cook County", "Harris County", /* ... */];
  const out = [];
  for (const n of names) {
    // The home region is a county, so origin=us_county (dest is what gets colored).
    const [hit] = await SCI.findRegions(n, { origin: "us_county", dest: "us_county" });
    if (!hit) continue;
    await SCI.generate({ origin: "us_county", dest: "us_county", source: hit.id });
    out.push({ name: n, png: await SCI.toBlob("png") });
  }

Notes / caveats:
- MP4 export needs the WebCodecs H.264 encoder. It works in a real (headed)
  browser but may be unavailable in a headless one — prefer PNG/JPG/SVG for
  headless pipelines.
- Region ids are opaque (FIPS like 06037, GADM ids like IND.34.75_1). Use
  `source` when you know the id; otherwise `sourceName` / SCI.findRegions().
  Name lookup is accent-insensitive and also matches curated aliases from
  /data/geo/aliases.json, so common alternate names like "Brussels", "Mumbai",
  "Bengaluru", or "Bozen" can resolve to the canonical displayed region names.
- The raw exported data is also fetchable as static JSON under /data/
  (manifest.json, groups.json, palettes.json, countries.json, bounds.json,
  geo/<level>_names.json, geo/aliases.json, sci/<type>/<id>.json) if you'd rather
  compute directly. CGFR runtime data is under /data/cgfr/.
